description1: After graduating from the University of Arkansas in 2020 with bachelor’s degrees in Physics and Mechanical Engineering, I attended Officer Candidate School. There, I spent 3 months training to be a Naval Officer. I then commissioned in September of 2020, and moved to Charleston, SC to begin my nuclear training.
description2: My nuclear training consisted of nuclear power school and nuclear prototype training. Power school was 6 months of classroom studying and test taking. We covered math, physics, reactor design, chemistry, reactor theory, heat transfer, and principles of reactor operations. In prototype training, I spent an additional 6 months on shift work learning how to operate a nuclear reactor. This portion of my training included hands on operations as well as verbal boards to certify my knowledge of nuclear power. I conducted multiple reactor start-ups and shut-downs, as well as several casualty drills.
description3: Following my nuclear training, I spent the next 3 years on a ballistic missile submarine. While on board, I ran multiple divisions, qualified in submarines, operated a nuclear reactor, and spent hundreds of hours driving the submarine. I ran audit and surveillance programs, trained enlisted and officers, and ran several programs. Today, I work at USSTRATCOM conducting mission planning and providing subject matter expertise on submarines.
